title: News | Suella Braverman
h1: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
headings: h2: Scrap the Family Farm Tax
h2: Ending DEI at the FA
h2: Tea and cake in Denmead
text_excerpt: --> News | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Join Suella's email list and receive her latest news and monthly newsletter straight to your inbox. Enter your email here Sign Up London Road HMO 13th April 2026 ​ ​ I’m very concerned by the decision of the Labour‑Lib Dem‑Green‑run Havant Borough Council to approve a 12-bed HMO planning application in Waterlooville. It has not been ruled out that this could be used for asylum seekers, and it’s another example of local politicians putting ideology before the interests of residents. They should never have approved it, and local people have every right to feel frustrated and ignored. ​ Well done and thank you to Reform UK Councillor Gwen Robinson for standing up for our community and opposing this damaging proposal. Gwen represents exactly what local politics should be about: integrity, accountability, and a genuine commitment to putting residents first. ​ On May 7th, you have the chance to send a clear message that enough is enough. It’s time to bring back common sense, competence, and courage to Havant Borough Council. Vote Reform to put local people, not party politics, back in charge! Happy Easter! 5th April 2026 ​ We are so lucky to have some truly beautiful churches here in Fareham and Waterlooville, places of peace, reflection, and community. ​ Easter is always a special time to come together with family and friends, to celebrate hope, renewal, and faith. It’s also a reminder of our country’s deep Christian roots with traditions that have shaped our values, guided our institutions, and given us a shared sense of purpose for centuries. ​ I’m proud that Britain is a Christian country and that our churches continue to play such a vital role in local life, bringing people together, supporting those in need, and keeping faith alive for generations to come. ​ I Hope you all had a wonderful Easter surrounded by loved ones! Hopscotch in Fareham! 2nd April 2026 ​ Very excited that Hopscotch Day Nurseries will be opening a new nursery in Fareham at the old Ashcroft Arts Centre later this year. ​ I’m pleased to have supported this initiative last year and to have worked with Hampshire County Council and the team to help move this forward. ​ This will be a great community asset! ​ Watch my video on Facebook here! - https://www.facebook.com/share/r/1KeUKiJTC5/ Fix the potholes! 30th March 2026 ​ I’ll keep urging Hampshire County Council to fix the potholes. ​ Several residents have complained to me about the state of roads in different parts of Fareham & Waterlooville, like London Road in Waterlooville and Wynton Way in Fareham where I saw loads recently. ​ You pay your Council Tax and deserve better! ​ ​ Scrap the Family Farm Tax 27th March 2026 ​ Good meeting today at Rookesbury Park with the Country Land and Business Association and local farmers from Fareham and Waterlooville. It was a valuable opportunity to listen to those who know our countryside best and understand the realities of running farms and rural businesses day to day. ​ We spoke about the challenges facing British farming from rising costs and regulation to the need for long‑term investment and sustainable food production. Our farmers work tirelessly to feed the nation, care for the land, and support local jobs, and their contributions deserve both recognition and support. ​ I’ll continue working with local landowners and the CLA to champion rural communities, protect our farmland, and ensure British agriculture remains at the heart of a strong, self‑reliant nation. ​ ​ ​ Ending DEI at the FA 24th March 2026 The Football Association wants to mandate that 1 in 4 football coaches come from a Black, Asian or other minority background. As the saying in football goes, this is utter woke nonsense. The game’s gone. ​ F
